concept: Impatient / Unable to Wait / Restless for Action
description: Having or showing a tendency to be quickly irritated or provoked; restlessly eager. 'Sheli' names the quality of impatience—the inability to wait, the urge to rush.
sound_symbolism: The word pushes forward. 'She' /ʃe/ begins with pressed breath, waiting difficult. Then 'li' moves quickly, patience exhausted.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'sheli miona' (impatient person). Opposite of 'hurao' (patient). Names state that often causes harm through rushing.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Impatience is aversion to the present moment—wanting things to be other than they are, faster than they are.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Impatient communication interrupts and rushes—not allowing others time to think or speak fully.
